marketState: neutral - bullish hot-weather tilt near-term, bearish storage/seasonality tilt further out
summary: Front-month NG at $2.923 is trading exactly at modeled fair value (band $2.42-$3.42), so there is no near-term valuation edge either way. The clearest single signal is weather: 1-7D and 8-16D demand run +20.1% and +17.8% above the 5-year normal with range positions of 0.81 and 0.91, a genuinely bullish cooling-demand pulse that supports the front month, but that forecast only covers 16 days - a small slice of a 3-12 week horizon. Beyond that window the fundamental and technical backdrop leans bearish: storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average after a bigger-than-forecast build (surprise +22 Bcf, bearish), seasonality gives only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks with a -1.4% typical move (price already -7.3pp below its typical-year path, though reliability is explicitly Low), daily/weekly technicals remain bearish (50<200 EMA, RSI ~38, negative MACD), and managed money is net short with room to extend (35th percentile of its 5-year range). Net: lean long into the current hot-weather pulse over the next 1-10 days, but fade strength on a longer horizon where the storage surplus and weak seasonal odds dominate.

longTerm.entry: $3.00-$3.10 (sell a rally toward the plus14D/plus21D fair-value line, fair $2.998-$3.064)
longTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, seasonality reliability flagged Low)
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise, seasonality gives only 38% odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out with a -1.4% typical move, daily/weekly technicals are bearish (50<200 EMA, RSI ~38, negative MACD), and managed money is already net short with room to add (35th percentile). The bullish weather read only spans 16 days versus a much longer 3-12 week window, so it shouldn't dominate this horizon despite its priority ranking.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.30, a storage deficit emerging versus the 5-year average, or the weekly technical trend flipping bullish (MACD positive, price back above the 50-week EMA) would invalidate this view.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D and 8-16D cooling demand are running well above 5-year norms (+20.1%/+17.8%, range position 0.81/0.91), supporting spot strength through peak cooling season, and next-month trades at a discount to front (mild backwardation) consistent with prompt tightness. Price sits exactly at fair value with room toward the rising plus7D/plus14D fair levels (~$2.95-$3.00), and intraday technicals (5m/15m, RSI ~62, 50>200 EMA) already show a bullish tilt even as higher timeframes stay weak.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained daily close below $2.78, or the 1-7D/8-16D weather demand anomaly fading back toward the 5-year normal, would invalidate this view.

Price exactly at fair value now ($2.923 vs $2.923 fair); managed money net short at the 35th percentile, room to extend
risks: Weather forecasts, especially the 8-16D window, can shift quickly and remove the near-term bullish catalyst
